data.content:
I'm building a demo of a retail store operations management platform — 
a React SPA hosted on GitHub Pages, all synthetic data, no backend. 

The concept: a "Store Operations Copilot" that helps HQ, regional managers 
and store managers convert operational signals into prioritised daily actions, 
track execution with proof/evidence, and escalate exceptions.

Research the best solutions in the market that allow for this, to get a detail requirements specifications. There are multiple vendors like those listed here: https://axonify.com/blog/retail-execution-platforms/ and things like Quorso. 

I need to provide a 15 minute demo, so I need a POC/ MVP I can show then. It needs to work offline as a SPA, with relevant mock data. Eg: Current PS% promotions include.... Come up with some really relevant features for this, after researching thoroughly.

Before writing any code, give me a project plan: architecture, 
data model, routes, component breakdown, and build order and the feature set that we will demo
I'll research the retail execution platform market first, then build out a detailed project plan. Let me pull from the sources you mentioned and a couple of leaders.

toolRequests.name: fetch_webpage
toolRequests.arguments: {"urls": ["https://axonify.com/blog/retail-execution-platforms/"], "query": "retail execution platform features task management store operations daily actions"}

toolRequests.name: fetch_webpage
toolRequests.arguments: {"urls": ["https://www.quorso.com/"], "query": "store operations management daily actions prioritised signals KPIs missions"}

data.reasoningText: I need to research retail execution platforms and map out a comprehensive project plan before diving into any code. Since I'm in read-only mode, I'll focus on understanding the market landscape and the specific requirements from the linked resources to build a solid foundation for the plan.

arguments.query: retail execution platform features task management store operations daily actions
store operations management daily actions prioritised signals KPIs missions

data.content:
Research done. Here's the full project plan — no code yet, just the blueprint. I've grounded the feature set in what the market leaders (Axonify, Quorso, Yoobic, Reflexis, StoreForce) actually do, then trimmed to what's demoable offline in 15 minutes.

---

## 1. Market research → what these platforms actually do

**The core problem they all solve:** the gap between what HQ plans and what happens on the floor. Only ~48% of plans get executed as intended; out-of-stocks alone drive ~$1T in losses. The platforms close that "knowing → doing" gap.

**Common capability pillars (synthesised across vendors):**

| Pillar | Vendors emphasising it | What it means for our demo |
|---|---|---|
| **Signal → prioritised action** ("Missions") | Quorso, Reflexis | Turn KPI exceptions into a ranked daily action list per manager |
| **Task management + digital checklists + escalation** | Axonify, Yoobic, Reflexis | Recurring/one-off tasks, due dates, auto-escalation on overdue |
| **Proof / evidence capture** | GoSpotCheck, Repsly, Yoobic | Photo upload, checklist completion, notes as execution proof |
| **Compliance & audits** (planogram, promo, safety) | Repsly, Bitreport, Wiser | "Perfect store" scoring, promo compliance % |
| **Performance analytics — store/region/enterprise rollup** | StoreForce, Quorso | Dashboards, completion rates, compliance trends, sales lift |
| **Targeted frontline comms** | Axonify, Yoobic | Role/location/shift-targeted messages |
| **Tie execution to outcomes** | Quorso | Link compliance scores → sales / shrink / CSAT |

**Quorso's specific model** (closest to your concept): unifies siloed data → **prioritised daily "Missions"** per manager → tools to act → results flow back so "every cycle compounds." Three personas: **Store Manager** (all my work in one place), **District/Regional Manager** (improve every store every day), **Central Operations** (personalised window into stores). That maps exactly to your HQ / regional / store hierarchy.

---

## 2. Demo feature set (what we'll show in 15 minutes)

I'm scoping to **6 hero features** that tell a complete story and survive a live demo. Everything is synthetic and runs offline.

**F1 — Role switcher (HQ ▸ Regional ▸ Store Manager).** One click reframes the entire app to that persona's scope and priorities. Sells the multi-tier story instantly.

**F2 — Signals engine.** Synthetic operational signals derived from KPIs per store: on-shelf availability (OSA) drops, **promo compliance / PS% gaps**, shrink spikes, sales-vs-target, waste/spoilage, CSAT dips, labour variance. Each signal has severity, impacted £ value, and trend.

**F3 — Prioritised Daily Actions ("Missions").** The signals are converted into a ranked action list per role/store, scored by impact × urgency × effort. This is the centrepiece — "convert operational signals into prioritised daily actions."

**F4 — Action execution + Proof/Evidence.** Open an action → checklist steps, assignee, due time, attach photo evidence (mock image), add note, mark complete. Status flows: Open → In Progress → Done/Verified. Demonstrates "track execution with proof."

**F5 — Exception escalation.** Overdue or high-severity actions auto-flag and escalate up the hierarchy (Store → Regional → HQ) with a visible escalation trail. Demonstrates "escalate exceptions."

**F6 — Performance rollup dashboard.** Store → Region → Enterprise aggregation: execution completion %, promo compliance trend, sales lift attributable to closed actions, leaderboard of stores. Closes the loop ("results flow back, every cycle compounds").

**Stretch (only if time):** targeted comms/broadcast, "Perfect Store" audit checklist with score, promo calendar.

### The metrics/KPI vocabulary we'll use (makes it credible)
- **Sales vs Target** (£), **Conversion %**, **Basket/ATV**
- **PS% (Promotional Sales %)** — share of revenue from active promos; plus **Promo Compliance %** (displays set up correctly)
- **OSA % (On-Shelf Availability)** / out-of-stock count
- **Shrink %**, **Waste/Spoilage %**
- **CSAT / NPS**, **Mystery shopper / Audit score**
- **Labour: schedule adherence %**

Example signal copy: *"Promo PS% on 'Summer BBQ' bundle is 4.2% vs 9.0% target at Manchester Arndale — display compliance failed audit. Est. impact £3.1k/wk."*

---

## 3. Architecture

**Stack** (chosen for offline SPA on GitHub Pages):
- **React 18 + TypeScript + Vite** — fast, static build, GH-Pages friendly
- **React Router v6 with `HashRouter`** — avoids GH Pages 404 on deep links (no server rewrites)
- **Zustand** for state (lightweight, no boilerplate) + **localStorage persistence** so actions/escalations survive refresh during the demo
- **Tailwind CSS** + a few **shadcn/ui**-style components for a polished, professional look fast
- **Recharts** for trend/rollup charts
- **lucide-react** for icons
- **Seeded synthetic data** generated at build/load time with a deterministic PRNG (e.g. `mulberry32`) so the demo looks identical every run
- **No backend, no network calls.** All data lives in `/src/data` and in-memory store. Optional PWA manifest + service worker for true "installable/offline."

**Why these choices:** everything compiles to static assets, the hash router guarantees deep links work on `username.github.io/repo/#/...`, and seeded data means your live demo never surprises you.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Seed data + PRNG] --> B[Domain layer: stores, KPIs, signals]
    B --> C[Signal engine: KPI vs target -> Signal objects]
    C --> D[Action generator: Signals -> ranked Actions/Missions]
    D --> E[Zustand store + localStorage]
    E --> F[Role context: HQ / Regional / Store scope filter]
    F --> G[UI: routes, dashboards, action detail, escalation]
    G -->|complete/escalate| E
```

**Layering:** keep pure logic (`/lib`: signal engine, scoring, rollups) separate from React so it's testable and the demo logic is obvious.

---

## 4. Data model

Core entities (TypeScript shapes, conceptual):

```
Region        { id, name, managerId }
Store         { id, name, regionId, managerId, format, city }
User          { id, name, role: 'hq'|'regional'|'store', regionId?, storeId? }

KPI           { id, storeId, metric: 'PS'|'OSA'|'Shrink'|'Sales'|'CSAT'|
                'Waste'|'Conversion'|'LabourAdherence',
                value, target, unit, trend: number[], periodEnd }

Promotion     { id, name, mechanic, startDate, endDate,
                targetPSPct, storeIds[], complianceByStore }

Signal        { id, storeId, sourceMetric, severity: 'low'|'med'|'high'|'critical',
                title, description, impactValue (£), trendDir, detectedAt }

Action(Mission){ id, signalId?, storeId, regionId, title, category,
                 priorityScore, impactValue, effort, dueAt,
                 status: 'open'|'in_progress'|'done'|'verified'|'escalated',
                 assigneeId, checklist: ChecklistItem[],
                 evidence: Evidence[], escalation?: Escalation, createdAt }

ChecklistItem { id, label, done }
Evidence      { id, type: 'photo'|'note', value, addedBy, addedAt }
Escalation    { fromRole, toRole, reason, raisedAt, history[] }

AuditTemplate { id, name, sections[] }  // "Perfect Store" (stretch)
Message       { id, audience, body, sentBy, sentAt }  // comms (stretch)
```

**Priority score formula** (the brain of the "Missions" feature):
`priorityScore = (impactValue_normalised × severityWeight × urgencyDecay) / effortWeight`
where urgency rises as `dueAt` approaches. This makes the ranked list defensible on stage.

**Rollup logic:** Store KPIs → aggregate to Region (weighted by store sales) → aggregate to Enterprise. Execution metrics (completion %, escalation count) roll up the same way.

---

## 5. Routes (HashRouter)

```
/                         Redirect → role's home
/login (optional)         Pick persona (or use top-bar role switcher)

# HQ / Enterprise
/hq                       Enterprise dashboard (all regions rollup)
/hq/regions               Region leaderboard
/hq/escalations           Escalations inbox (cross-region)

# Regional
/region/:regionId         Region dashboard (store leaderboard)
/region/:regionId/stores  Store list with KPI heatmap

# Store
/store/:storeId           Store home: today's prioritised actions
/store/:storeId/signals   Signals feed
/store/:storeId/promos    Active promotions + PS%/compliance

# Shared
/actions                  My prioritised actions (scoped to role)
/actions/:actionId        Action detail: checklist, evidence, escalate
/analytics                Trends & execution-vs-outcome charts
/audits/:storeId          Perfect Store audit (stretch)
/comms                    Broadcast/targeted messages (stretch)
```

The **role switcher** in the top bar changes scope and redirects to the right home, so you can pivot personas live without re-navigating.

---

## 6. Component breakdown

**Layout / shell**
- `AppShell` (sidebar + topbar), `RoleSwitcher`, `ScopeBreadcrumb`, `NotificationBell` (escalations)

**Dashboards**
- `EnterpriseDashboard`, `RegionDashboard`, `StoreHome`
- `KpiCard`, `KpiHeatmap`, `StoreLeaderboard`, `TrendChart` (Recharts), `RollupBar`

**Signals & Actions (hero)**
- `SignalsFeed`, `SignalCard` (severity chip, £ impact, sparkline)
- `ActionList` (sorted by priorityScore), `ActionCard`, `PriorityBadge`
- `ActionDetailPanel` → `ChecklistEditor`, `EvidenceUploader` (mock photo), `NoteThread`, `StatusStepper`, `EscalateButton`

**Escalation**
- `EscalationInbox`, `EscalationTrail`, `EscalationModal`

**Promotions**
- `PromoList`, `PromoCard` (PS% actual vs target gauge), `ComplianceBadge`

**Analytics**
- `ExecutionVsOutcomeChart` (completion % vs sales lift), `CompletionTrend`, `ComplianceTrend`

**Primitives**
- `Button`, `Card`, `Badge`, `Modal`, `Tabs`, `Gauge`, `EmptyState`, `Avatar`

**State / logic (non-React)**
- `lib/prng.ts`, `lib/signalEngine.ts`, `lib/actionGenerator.ts`, `lib/rollups.ts`, `lib/scoring.ts`
- `store/useAppStore.ts` (Zustand + persist), `store/roleContext.ts`
- `data/seed.ts` (regions, stores, users, promos, KPIs)

---

## 7. Build order (so there's always a runnable demo)

1. **Scaffold** — Vite + TS + Tailwind + Router (HashRouter) + Zustand; deploy a "hello" to GH Pages early to de-risk hosting.
2. **Data + domain** — seed regions/stores/users/promos/KPIs with seeded PRNG; build `signalEngine` + `actionGenerator` + `scoring`. *(Pure logic, no UI — verify in console.)*
3. **App shell + RoleSwitcher** — sidebar/topbar, role context, scoped routing.
4. **Store Home + ActionList** (hero #1) — prioritised daily actions render for a store. This alone is demoable.
5. **ActionDetail + Evidence + checklist + status** (hero #2) — execution with proof; persist to localStorage.
6. **Escalation flow** (hero #3) — overdue/high-severity → escalate → inbox + trail.
7. **Region + Enterprise dashboards + rollups** (hero #4) — leaderboards, heatmap, trend charts.
8. **Promotions / PS% view** — promo compliance + PS% gauges feeding signals.
9. **Analytics: execution → outcome** — the "compounding loop" chart.
10. **Polish** — empty states, demo reset button, seeded "today," PWA/offline manifest, final styling pass.
11. **Stretch** — comms broadcast, Perfect Store audit.

A **"Reset demo data" button** and a fixed synthetic "today" are worth building in step 2 so every run-through is clean.

---

## Suggested 15-minute demo narrative
1. **(1m)** Problem: HQ plans vs store reality, the £ gap.
2. **(2m)** Land as **Store Manager** → today's prioritised actions, lead with the PS%/promo-compliance one.
3. **(3m)** Open an action → checklist, attach photo proof, mark done.
4. **(2m)** A critical OSA signal → escalate to Regional.
5. **(2m)** Switch to **Regional** → store leaderboard, escalation inbox, accept/act.
6. **(3m)** Switch to **HQ** → enterprise rollup, execution-vs-sales-lift "compounding loop."
7. **(2m)** Recap: signals → prioritised actions → proof → escalation → measurable outcome.

---

Want me to proceed with **step 1 (scaffold + GH Pages deploy)** and **step 2 (seed data + signal engine)** next? I can also adjust the persona names, retailer flavour (grocery vs specialty vs convenience), and currency (£/$/€) before we start so the synthetic data feels right for your audience.

I'll set up the tech stack with React, Vite, and TypeScript, using HashRouter for GitHub Pages compatibility, Zustand for state management, mock data in JSON, Recharts for visualizations, and Tailwind for styling—plus localStorage for persistence and a seedable RNG to keep the demo data consistent. Now I'll structure the 15-minute demo flow to walk through the key features.

data.content:
Research done. Here's the full project plan — no code yet, just the blueprint for us to align on before building.

# Store Operations Copilot — Project Plan

## 1. Market research → what the demo must prove

From the Axonify roundup and Quorso (plus Yoobic, Reflexis/Zebra, GoSpotCheck, Repsly), the category converges on a clear pattern. The winning idea — and the one your brief already names — is **Quorso's "Missions" model**: take a mountain of operational data, turn it into a short, *prioritised* list of daily actions per manager, help them act, capture proof, and measure the impact (closing the loop).

**Capabilities the leaders share (our feature shortlist is drawn from these):**

| Capability | Vendors | In our demo? |
|---|---|---|
| Signal → prioritised daily actions ("Missions") | Quorso | ✅ Core |
| Task management + digital checklists + escalation | Axonify, Yoobic, Reflexis | ✅ Core |
| Photo / evidence-based proof of execution | GoSpotCheck, Repsly, Yoobic | ✅ Core |
| Planogram / promo / price compliance | Repsly (AI image), Wiser | ✅ (mocked verify) |
| Multi-tier visibility (HQ → Region → Store) | Quorso, Reflexis | ✅ Core (3 personas) |
| Performance analytics tying execution → sales/shrink | Quorso, StoreForce | ✅ Core |
| Frontline comms / targeted messaging | Axonify, Yoobic | ⚠️ Light (escalation thread) |
| Just-in-time training | Axonify, Yoobic | ⚠️ Stretch (link only) |
| Offline / mobile-first | All | ✅ (SPA works offline by design) |

**The three problems the demo narrative attacks** (straight from the research):
1. The strategy↔execution gap — HQ plans a PS5 promo; stores execute inconsistently.
2. Blind spots — HQ can't see what's actually happening on the floor in real time.
3. Proving ROI — connecting execution (display up, stock replenished) to sales lift.

---

## 2. Demo feature set (what we'll show in 15 minutes)

A retailer we'll call **"Voltly"** — a consumer-electronics & gaming chain (perfect for the PS5 storyline).

**F1 — Today's Missions (Store Manager home).** Prioritised, ranked action cards generated from signals. Each card: title, why-it-matters (the signal + £ impact), priority score, due time, steps, evidence required.

**F2 — Mission detail + proof of execution.** Checklist steps, "upload photo" (mock), capture a count/price, mark complete → mission moves to Done and updates a KPI.

**F3 — Signals engine (the "Copilot" brain).** A transparent rules layer that converts raw signals into missions. We *show* the logic: e.g. *"PS5 Spider-Man 2 bundle promo live, but Store #214 sold 0 units in 2 days AND display compliance = unverified → generate high-priority 'Set up PS5 endcap & verify' mission."*

**F4 — Regional Manager cockpit.** League table of stores, exceptions surfaced, drill into a store, escalations inbox, one-click "nudge store" / reassign.

**F5 — HQ / Central Ops campaign view.** Launch/track the PS5 promo across all stores: compliance heatmap, adoption %, sales lift vs target, exceptions rolling up.

**F6 — Exceptions & escalation flow.** A mission that's overdue or blocked (e.g. "PS5 stock = 0, can't action") escalates from Store → Region → HQ with a status thread.

**F7 — Impact / "close the loop" panel.** Before/after on a KPI when missions get completed — e.g. attach-rate of DualSense controllers rises after the bundle endcap goes up.

**Demo "wow" anchor — the PS5 storyline** runs through all three personas: HQ launches the *PS5 Slim "Spider-Man 2" bundle £40-off* promo → signals detect 4 stores not converting → missions fire → a store manager completes the endcap with photo proof → impact panel shows the lift → one store has zero stock → escalation to region/HQ.

---

## 3. Architecture

Constraints: React SPA, GitHub Pages, **100% client-side, synthetic data, offline-capable.**

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph Browser["Browser (SPA — works offline)"]
        UI[React UI / Routes]
        Store[State: Zustand store]
        Engine[Signals→Missions rules engine]
        Seed[Static seed data JSON/TS]
        Persist[localStorage persistence]
    end
    Seed --> Store
    Engine --> Store
    Store <--> Persist
    Store --> UI
    UI -->|user actions| Store
    Store -->|recompute KPIs| Engine
```

**Stack decision:**
- **Vite + React + TypeScript** — fast, simple, ideal for GitHub Pages (`base` path config).
- **React Router** (`HashRouter` — avoids GitHub Pages 404 deep-link issues, no 404.html hack).
- **State: Zustand** (lightweight) + persist middleware → mutations survive refresh and seed is reset-able for a clean demo.
- **Styling: Tailwind CSS** + a few **Recharts** charts. Optionally shadcn/ui for polished cards/tables.
- **Data: TypeScript seed modules** (typed, importable) — no backend, no fetch. Everything is in-bundle so it works fully offline.
- **No auth** — a persona switcher in the top bar (HQ / Region / Store) to fake role context.
- **Resettable**: a "Reset demo" button restores seed → critical for re-running a live demo.

The "Copilot/AI" is a **deterministic rules engine** (transparent, demo-safe). We frame it as the intelligence layer; optionally show a faked natural-language "Ask the Copilot" box returning canned, data-grounded answers. No live LLM = no network = reliable on stage.

---

## 4. Data model

```mermaid
erDiagram
    REGION ||--o{ STORE : contains
    STORE ||--o{ STORE_KPI : has
    STORE ||--o{ INVENTORY : holds
    PRODUCT ||--o{ INVENTORY : stocked_as
    PROMOTION ||--o{ PROMO_PRODUCT : includes
    PRODUCT ||--o{ PROMO_PRODUCT : in
    SIGNAL ||--o{ MISSION : generates
    STORE ||--o{ MISSION : assigned_to
    MISSION ||--o{ MISSION_STEP : has
    MISSION ||--o{ EVIDENCE : proven_by
    MISSION ||--o{ ESCALATION : raises
    USER ||--o{ MISSION : owns
```

**Core entities (fields abbreviated):**

- **User** — id, name, role (`HQ` | `RegionalManager` | `StoreManager`), regionId?, storeId?.
- **Region** — id, name, managerUserId.
- **Store** — id, name, code (#214), regionId, format (Flagship/High St/Mall), managerUserId.
- **Product** — id, sku, name, category (Console/Game/Accessory/TV…), price. *(PS5 Slim, PS5 Pro, DualSense Edge, Spider-Man 2, FIFA, Pulse 3D headset, 65" OLED…)*
- **Promotion** — id, name, description, type (Bundle/Price-cut/Trade-in), startDate, endDate, mechanic, targetUplift. *(e.g. "PS5 Slim × Spider-Man 2 Bundle – £40 off")*
- **PromoProduct** — promotionId, productId.
- **Inventory** — storeId, productId, onHand, onOrder, status (`InStock`|`Low`|`OOS`).
- **StoreKPI** — storeId, date, salesVsTarget%, conversion%, attachRate%, oosRate%, complianceScore%, shrink%.
- **Signal** — id, type, severity, storeId, productId?, promotionId?, value, threshold, detectedAt, message. **Types:** `PromoUnderperforming`, `OutOfStock`, `DisplayUnverified`, `PriceMismatch`, `LowAttachRate`, `ShrinkAnomaly`, `StaffingGap`, `SafetyCheckDue`, `NegativeFeedback`.
- **Mission** — id, title, rationale, priorityScore, status (`Todo`|`InProgress`|`Blocked`|`Done`|`Escalated`), dueAt, storeId, ownerUserId, sourceSignalId, estImpactGBP, category, evidenceRequired(bool).
- **MissionStep** — missionId, order, label, type (`check`|`photo`|`count`|`price`), done.
- **Evidence** — missionId, type (`photo`|`note`|`count`), value/imageRef, capturedAt, capturedBy.
- **Escalation** — id, missionId, fromLevel, toLevel, reason, status, thread[].

**Signals→Missions engine (rules, illustrative):**
- `PromoUnderperforming` + `DisplayUnverified` → "Build & verify PS5 endcap" (High).
- `OutOfStock` on promo SKU → "Replenish PS5 stock / raise transfer" → auto-`Blocked`→`Escalated` if onOrder = 0.
- `PriceMismatch` → "Correct shelf price label" (Med).
- `LowAttachRate` on consoles → "Place DualSense + Pulse 3D at till / bundle prompt" (Med).
- `SafetyCheckDue` → recurring compliance checklist (Low/standing).
Priority score = `severity × estImpactGBP × deadline proximity`.

---

## 5. Routes (HashRouter)

| Route | Persona | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | all | Persona switcher / redirect to role home |
| `/store` | Store Mgr | **Today's Missions** dashboard (F1) |
| `/store/mission/:id` | Store Mgr | Mission detail + proof (F2) |
| `/store/store-health` | Store Mgr | This store's KPIs |
| `/region` | Regional Mgr | League table + exceptions cockpit (F4) |
| `/region/store/:id` | Regional Mgr | Drill-down into one store |
| `/region/escalations` | Regional Mgr | Escalations inbox (F6) |
| `/hq` | HQ | Campaign command centre (F5) |
| `/hq/campaign/:id` | HQ | PS5 promo: compliance heatmap, lift, exceptions |
| `/hq/signals` | HQ | Signals engine explorer (F3 — show the logic) |
| `/impact` | all | Close-the-loop impact panel (F7) |

Top bar: persona switcher, "Reset demo", optional "Ask Copilot" box.

---

## 6. Component breakdown

**Layout/shared:** `AppShell`, `TopBar` (persona switch, reset), `SideNav`, `PriorityBadge`, `KpiStat`, `StatusPill`, `EvidenceThumb`, `EmptyState`.

**Store Manager:** `MissionList`, `MissionCard`, `MissionDetail`, `StepChecklist`, `EvidenceCapture` (mock photo/count/price), `CompleteMissionButton`, `StoreHealthCards`.

**Regional Manager:** `StoreLeagueTable` (sortable), `ExceptionFeed`, `StoreDrilldown`, `EscalationInbox`, `NudgeStoreAction`.

**HQ:** `CampaignHeader`, `ComplianceHeatmap` (store × step grid), `AdoptionChart`, `SalesLiftChart` (Recharts), `SignalsExplorer` (rule → generated missions), `ExceptionRollup`.

**Engine/data:** `signalsEngine.ts` (rules), `kpiCalculator.ts`, `seed/*.ts`, `store.ts` (Zustand), `useDemoReset()`.

**Cross-cutting:** `ImpactPanel` (before/after), `AskCopilot` (canned grounded answers), `CopilotRationale` (explains why a mission exists).

---

## 7. Build order

**Phase 0 — Scaffold (foundation)**
1. Vite + React + TS + Tailwind + Router + Zustand; GitHub Pages `base`/`HashRouter`; deploy a "hello" to Pages early to de-risk hosting.

**Phase 1 — Data + engine (the brain)**
2. Type definitions for all entities.
3. Seed data: ~12 stores / 3 regions, ~20 products incl. PS5 line, the PS5 promo, inventory, KPIs.
4. `signalsEngine` rules + mission generation; `kpiCalculator`. Verify missions appear from seed.

**Phase 2 — Store Manager flow (primary demo path)**
5. `MissionList` + `MissionCard` (priority, rationale, £ impact).
6. `MissionDetail` + `StepChecklist` + `EvidenceCapture` + complete → status & KPI update. *This is the core "aha".*

**Phase 3 — Regional cockpit**
7. League table + exception feed + store drilldown.
8. Escalation flow (blocked → escalated thread).

**Phase 4 — HQ campaign centre**
9. Campaign view: compliance heatmap, adoption, sales-lift chart.
10. Signals explorer (show the logic transparently).

**Phase 5 — Close the loop + polish**
11. `ImpactPanel` before/after; optional `AskCopilot`.
12. Reset-demo button, empty states, mobile responsiveness, deploy.

**Phase 6 — Demo dress rehearsal**
13. Seed tuned so the PS5 narrative lands cleanly; rehearse the 15-min script.

---

## 8. 15-minute demo script (the narrative)

1. **(2 min) HQ** launches/monitors the *PS5 Slim × Spider-Man 2 £40-off bundle*. "Live in 12 stores — but look, 4 stores aren't converting and 3 displays are unverified." Show compliance heatmap.
2. **(2 min) Signals explorer** — "Here's *why* the Copilot flagged it" — the rule that turns the underperformance + unverified-display signals into missions. Establishes trust/transparency.
3. **(4 min) Store Manager (#214)** — opens app, sees today's *prioritised* missions, top one = "Build & verify PS5 endcap (£3,200 at risk)." Works the checklist, uploads a photo proof, confirms stock count → marks done → KPI ticks up.
4. **(3 min) Exception** — a second store has PS5 stock = 0; mission auto-**blocks** and **escalates** to Region → HQ with a thread. Shows the escalation path.
5. **(2 min) Regional Manager** — league table, store #214 jumps up the ranking, exception inbox, one-click nudge to a lagging store.
6. **(2 min) Impact panel** — before/after: completed missions → conversion + DualSense attach-rate lift. Ties execution to ROI — the closing message.

---

## 9. Open questions before we build

1. **Brand:** OK to invent "Voltly" (electronics/gaming), or do you want a real-looking but generic name / a different vertical (grocery, fashion)? Electronics best fits the PS5 example.
2. **Persona depth:** all three personas fully, or go deepest on Store Manager + HQ and keep Regional lighter?
3. **UI library:** plain Tailwind, or add shadcn/ui for a more polished/enterprise look?
4. **"Ask Copilot" box:** include the faked NL query feature, or keep the rules-engine transparency as the only "AI" surface?
5. **Repo:** new repo for GitHub Pages, or build into this workspace folder?

If you're happy with this, tell me your answers to §9 and I'll switch into build mode and start Phase 0.

The user wants me to build this for a UK electronics retailer like Currys or Argos, keeping the design familiar and modern. They've specified a color palette with that distinctive blue-purple (#464feb) for links, light gray borders (#e6e6e6), and off-white table headers (#f5f5f5) — it's got that Microsoft/Copilot aesthetic. They're also expanding the feature set beyond what we started with. I'm noting the color palette the user provided — that indigo blue (#464feb) paired with light greys suggests a clean, modern enterprise interface. I should ground the visual design in Currys and Argos's actual brand languages to make the mockups feel authentic, so I'll research their sites for navigation patterns, product naming conventions, and design cues that will inform realistic mock data and UI styling.

For the feature set, I'm thinking through electronics retail operations more deeply — stock management around high-demand items like PS5s, promotional compliance and pricing, service attach rates for care plans and repairs, colleague support workflows, and compliance tracking. I should fetch both retailer sites in parallel to gather design and operational context, then synthesize that with the user's five operational themes to build out a more exhaustive and grounded feature list.

On the visual side, I'm considering a clean, familiar aesthetic with white backgrounds and light grey panels, using the accent color they specified. I should probably invent a fictional electronics retailer name to avoid trademark issues — something like "Voltly" that feels UK-retail-familiar but is clearly fictional. The architecture stays client-side SPA with role-based personas, but now expanded to handle the richer operational scenarios.

For the data model, I'm consolidating AI-generated and manually-created tasks into a single entity with a source field to distinguish them, and adding entities for knowledge snippets, escalations with SLA tracking, and evidence sources. The operational themes — stock, promo, service, compliance, colleague — should become a first-class taxonomy. I'll also need new routes for task orchestration, knowledge cards, and a sources panel that works as a slide-over available across the app.

Before I write the full plan, I should ground the design and product naming in real retail patterns, so I'm thinking about fetching some reference sites to inform the visual direction and brand feel.

Currys redirected to their ShopLive video widget (no usable markup), but Argos came through richly and confirms the real-world vocabulary I'll use to make this feel authentic: **Fast Track collection, Click & Collect, Argos Care (protection plans), Nectar loyalty, Argos Pay/Klarna BNPL, cashback codes, WOW deals/Clearance, returns & repairs**, plus categories like **Technology / Gaming / TV & soundbars / Appliances**. From Currys I'll borrow **Team Knowhow (repairs/setup), ShopLive (video expert), trade-in, price match, care & repair**. That's enough to ground both the UI and the mock data. Here's the revised, thorough plan. --- # Store Operations Copilot — Revised Plan (v2) ## 0. What changed from v1 - Vertical locked to **UK electronics & gaming retail**, styled to feel familiar to **Currys/Argos** shoppers and colleagues. - Feature set **expanded and mapped** to your 7 areas, plus electronics-specific operational scenarios and a few additions I recommend. Short answer to *"is that exhaustive?"* — it's a very strong 80%; I've added the missing 20% below (evidence capture as first-class, colleague comms/handover, store-walk audit, coaching, activity/audit trail). - New **design-system section** (your tokens baked in). - New **Source / Evidence panel** with the actual public sources used — this is the "based on public category research, not copied from Quorso" artifact. - Framed explicitly as **in-store software** (tablet/handheld for colleagues & store managers; desktop control-tower for region/HQ). --- ## 1. The five operational themes (the spine of everything) Your taxonomy becomes the core dimension every signal, task, KPI and filter hangs off: | Theme | Colour chip | Electronics in-store examples | |---|---|---| | **Stock** | Blue | OOS on hot SKUs (PS5), shop-floor vs stockroom mismatch, Click & Collect not picked, delivery/GNFR putaway | | **Promo** | Indigo | Endcap/display not built, price-label/ticket mismatch, promo live but 0 sales, competitor price-match | | **Service** | Teal | Care-plan/protection attach rate, Knowhow repairs backlog, trade-in, ShopLive, Click & Collect SLA, reviews/NPS | | **Compliance** | Amber | Age-restricted (PEGI 18) sales checks, electrical/PAT safety, fire & H&S, cash handling, opening/closing | | **Colleague support** | Purple | Staffing gaps on promo weekends, SOP/knowledge gaps, shift handover, new-starter onboarding | --- ## 2. Source / Evidence panel (public research the requirements are built from) A slide-over panel (available from the top bar on every screen) that renders this table. It's both a **feature** and your **credibility shield**. | Source | Type | What it informed | |---|---|---| | Axonify — "Top 10 retail execution software (2026)" | Public blog | Capability taxonomy: task mgmt + digital checklists, escalation workflows, comms, training, mobile/offline, analytics dashboards, POS/HRIS integration; the 5 common execution challenges | | Quorso (quorso.com) | Vendor site | "Missions" model: data → prioritised daily actions per manager; Store / District / Central personas; "close the loop" impact | | Currys (currys.co.uk) | Retailer site | Electronics service ops: Team Knowhow repairs/setup, ShopLive video expert, care & repair, trade-in, price match | | Argos (argos.co.uk) | Retailer site | Fast Track & Click & Collect, Argos Care protection, Nectar loyalty, BNPL, cashback/WOW deals, category & promo vocabulary | | Yoobic / Reflexis (Zebra) / GoSpotCheck / Repsly / StoreForce | Category leaders (named in Axonify) | Cross-reference for: frontline comms, labour+task integration, photo evidence/compliance audits, league tables/performance | Each requirement card in the app carries a small "source" tag linking back to a row here, so during the demo you can click any feature and show its public provenance. --- ## 3. Design system — "familiar like Currys/Argos, modern, easy" **Brand:** fictional retailer **"Voltly"** (electronics & gaming) — swappable single token. Alternatives if you prefer: *Currents*, *PowerHub*, *Brightwave Electricals*. **Design tokens (your CSS confirmed + extended):** | Token | Value | Use | |---|---|---| | `--accent` | `#464feb` | Links, primary buttons, active nav, "AI" accent | | `--accent-700` | `#2f37c9` | Hover/pressed | | `--border` | `#e6e6e6` | Table & card borders, dividers | | `--surface` | `#f5f5f5` | Table headers, panel backgrounds, app canvas | | `--bg` | `#ffffff` | Cards, content | | `--text` | `#1a1a1a` | Primary text | | `--text-muted` | `#616161` | Secondary text | | `--success` | `#1a7f37` | On-track, complete | | `--warning` | `#b88200` | At-risk, due soon | | `--danger` | `#c50f1f` | Breached, blocked, P1 | **Look & feel principles** (drawn from Currys/Argos + frontline-usability research): - Clean white cards on a light-grey canvas; **8–12px** radius; subtle 1px `--border`; minimal shadow. - **Pill badges** for status, priority and theme; data tables use your exact `th`/`td` border + `#f5f5f5` header. - System font stack (Segoe UI / Inter) — fast, modern, familiar. - **Glanceable & low-friction**: big tap targets, one primary action per card — these are frontline colleagues, not analysts. - **Two device modes:** *Store/colleague* views render inside a **tablet/phone frame** (sells "used inside the store"); *Region/HQ* views are full-width desktop **control towers**. - Fully **responsive + offline** (it's a static SPA). --- ## 4. Full feature set (exhaustive) — your 7 areas, expanded Legend: **[You]** = in your list · **[+]** = recommended addition · mapped to demo persona. ### F1 — Dashboard / Control Tower **[You]** — *HQ / Region* - **Estate health score** (0–100 composite: completion × compliance × stock availability × service SLA × colleague coverage), with trend arrow. - **Stores at risk** list (drivers shown), **overdue actions** count, **open exceptions/SLAs**. - **Operational-theme tiles** (Stock / Promo / Service / Compliance / Colleague) each with a health % and "jump to filtered list." - **[+]** Live **signals/alerts feed** and an **estate map/heatmap** (store × theme grid). ### F2 — AI Daily Priorities **[You]** — *all personas, store-scoped* - Ranked **priority cards**: **"Why this matters"** (the signal + threshold breached), **suggested action**, **expected impact (£ / KPI)**, **owner**, **due time**, **theme tag**, **source tag**. - Priority score = `severity × £impact × deadline-proximity` (transparent, shown on hover). - **[+]** "Snooze / accept / reassign" and a **Signals Explorer** that shows the *rule* that generated each priority (explainability = trust). ### F3 — Task Orchestration **[You]** — *HQ / Region create; Store executes* - **Create task**; **assign by store / region / role**; due date/time; theme; **evidence-required toggle**. - Status: **Not started · In progress · Blocked · Complete** (+ Escalated). - Unified model: AI priorities and manual tasks are the **same entity** with `source: ai | manual`. - **[+]** Recurring tasks (opening/closing), bulk-assign to a store cohort, acknowledgement/read receipts. ### F4 — Store Manager View **[You]** — *Store (tablet)* - **Daily brief** (today's priorities + promos live + staffing + weather/footfall note). - **Opening / closing checklist**; **department-specific tasks** (TV & Audio, Computing, Gaming, Mobile, Large Appliances, Smart Home). - **Knowledge snippets / SOP cards** (just-in-time: "How to set up the PS5 endcap", "PEGI age-check script", "Process a trade-in"). - **[+]** **Evidence capture** as first-class: photo (mock), count, price-check, signature/note — required when the toggle is on. - **[+]** **Shift handover** notes + colleague huddle/comms message of the day. ### F5 — Exception Handling **[You]** — *Store raises → Region/HQ* - **Blocked task** flow with reason; **escalate to IT / Facilities / Regional / Stock / Loss-Prevention**. - **Mock SLA status**: target window (e.g. 4h/24h) + computed **On-track / At-risk / Breached** against a seeded "now"; countdown chip. - **[+]** Escalation **thread** (status updates), and auto-escalation when an AI priority is `Blocked` (e.g. PS5 OOS, none on order). ### F6 — Analytics **[You]** — *HQ / Region* - **Completion rate**, **compliance trend**, **stock-exception trend** (Recharts). - **Stores needing coaching** (low completion/compliance + repeat exceptions). - **"Impact since morning"** summary: deltas vs a seeded AM snapshot (actions done, compliance ↑, stores moved out of "at risk", £ risk mitigated) — the **close-the-loop**. - **[+]** Per-theme breakdowns, store **league table**, and an **activity/audit trail** (who did what, when — backs the "proof/evidence" story). ### F7 — Source / Evidence Panel **[You]** — *global* - The slide-over from §2. Lists public sources + what each informed; every feature/requirement card deep-links to its source row. ### Electronics-specific scenarios baked into mock data (texture for the demo) PS5 Slim × *Spider-Man 2* £40-off **bundle** (anchor) · Galaxy/iPhone **trade-in + cashback** · 65" OLED **"big game" TV** promo (football season) · seasonal **fan/AC** stock surge · **Ring/smart-home attach** · **care-plan/Knowhow protection** attach-rate · **Click & Collect Fast-Track** SLA breach · **PEGI-18** age-restriction compliance · **electrical/PAT** safety check · **TV-dept staffing gap** on promo weekend. --- ## 5. Personas & "inside the store" usage model ```mermaid flowchart LR HQ["HQ / Central Ops
desktop control tower"] -->|launch promo, set tasks, watch estate| Region Region["Regional Manager
desktop cockpit"] -->|exceptions, coaching, nudges| Store Store["Store Manager + Colleagues
in-store tablet / handheld"] -->|execute, capture evidence, escalate| Region Region -->|rolls up signals & impact| HQ ``` - **HQ / Central Ops** — control tower, campaign launch, estate health, analytics. - **Regional Manager** — store league table, exceptions inbox, SLAs, coaching, nudges. - **Store Manager / Colleague** — *the in-store experience*: daily brief, prioritised tasks, checklists, SOP cards, evidence capture, escalate. Shown in a **tablet frame**. - Switching is via a **persona switcher** in the top bar (no real auth). **Reset demo** button restores seed. --- ## 6. Architecture ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Browser["Browser — static SPA, fully offline"] UI["React + Router UI
(control tower / store tablet modes)"] Store["Zustand store (+persist)"] Engine["Signals → Priorities rules engine"] KPI["KPI + Estate-health + Impact calculators"] SLA["SLA clock (seeded 'now')"] Seed["Typed seed data (TS modules)"] Sources["Sources registry"] end Seed --> Store Engine --> Store KPI --> Store SLA --> Store Sources --> UI Store <--> LS[(localStorage)] Store --> UI UI -->|complete / escalate / create| Store Store -->|recompute| KPI Store -->|recompute| Engine ``` - **Vite + React + TypeScript**, **HashRouter** (GitHub Pages deep-link safe), **Zustand + persist**, **Tailwind** with the tokens above, **Recharts** for trends. Optional **shadcn/ui** for polished tables/cards. - The **"AI"** is a transparent deterministic rules engine (demo-safe, no network). Optional canned **"Ask Voltly Copilot"** box as a stretch. - **Deterministic "now"** so SLA/impact states always look right on stage. --- ## 7. Data model ```mermaid erDiagram REGION ||--o{ STORE : contains STORE ||--o{ STORE_KPI : has STORE ||--o{ INVENTORY : holds PRODUCT ||--o{ INVENTORY : stocked_as PROMOTION ||--o{ PROMO_PRODUCT : includes PRODUCT ||--o{ PROMO_PRODUCT : in SIGNAL ||--o{ TASK : generates STORE ||--o{ TASK : assigned_to USER ||--o{ TASK : owns TASK ||--o{ TASK_STEP : has TASK ||--o{ EVIDENCE : proven_by TASK ||--o{ ESCALATION : raises ESCALATION ||--o{ ESCALATION_UPDATE : thread SOP ||--o{ TASK : guides SOURCE ||--o{ REQUIREMENT : substantiates ``` **Key entities** (abbreviated): `User`(role, regionId?, storeId?) · `Region` · `Store`(code #214, format, dept list) · `Product`(sku, name, category, price) · `Promotion`(name, mechanic, dates, targetUplift) · `Inventory`(onHand, onOrder, status) · `StoreKPI`(salesVsTarget, conversion, attachRate, oosRate, complianceScore, csat) · `Signal`(type, severity, theme, threshold, value) · **`Task`**(title, rationale/"why", source: ai|manual, theme, priorityScore, status, dueAt, ownerUserId, storeId, estImpactGBP, evidenceRequired) · `TaskStep`(label, type: check|photo|count|price) · `Evidence`(type, value/imageRef, by, at) · `Escalation`(target: IT|Facilities|Regional|Stock|LP, slaHours, startedAt, status) + `EscalationUpdate` thread · `SOP`(title, theme, body, dept) · `Source` + `Requirement`(for §2/§7). **Signal → Task rules (illustrative):** PromoUnderperforming + DisplayUnverified → *"Build & verify PS5 endcap"* (P1); OOS on promo SKU + onOrder=0 → *"Replenish PS5"* → auto-**Blocked→Escalated(Stock)**; PriceMismatch → *"Correct shelf ticket"* (P2); LowAttachRate → *"Prompt DualSense + care plan at till"* (P2); PEGI/PAT due → recurring compliance checklist (P3). --- ## 8. Routes / information architecture (HashRouter) | Route | Persona | Feature | |---|---|---| | `/` | all | Persona switcher / role redirect | | `/hq` | HQ | Control tower (F1) | | `/hq/campaign/:id` | HQ | Promo command centre (compliance heatmap, adoption, lift) | | `/hq/analytics` | HQ | Analytics (F6) | | `/hq/signals` | HQ | Signals Explorer (F2 explainability) | | `/region` | Region | Cockpit: league table + exceptions (F1/F5) | | `/region/store/:id` | Region | Store drilldown + coaching | | `/region/escalations` | Region | Escalations inbox + SLAs (F5) | | `/store` | Store | **Daily brief + AI priorities** (F2/F4) — tablet frame | | `/store/task/:id` | Store | Task detail + steps + **evidence** (F3/F4) | | `/store/checklists` | Store | Opening/closing + dept tasks (F4) | | `/store/knowledge` | Store | SOP / knowledge cards (F4) | | `/tasks/new` | HQ/Region | Create & assign task (F3) | | `/impact` | all | "Impact since morning" close-the-loop (F6) | Global top bar: persona switcher · **Reset demo** · **Sources panel** (F7) · optional Ask Copilot. --- ## 9. Component breakdown - **Shell/shared:** `AppShell`, `TopBar` (persona, reset, sources), `SideNav`, `DeviceFrame` (tablet/phone), `PriorityBadge`, `ThemeChip`, `StatusPill`, `SlaChip`, `KpiStat`, `Sparkline`, `EvidenceThumb`, `SourceTag`, `EmptyState`. - **Control tower (HQ/Region):** `EstateHealthGauge`, `StoresAtRiskList`, `ThemeHealthTiles`, `SignalsFeed`, `StoreLeagueTable`, `ComplianceHeatmap`, `ExceptionInbox`, `SlaTracker`, charts (`CompletionTrend`, `ComplianceTrend`, `StockExceptionTrend`), `CoachingList`. - **Store (tablet):** `DailyBrief`, `PriorityCard` ("why/action/impact/owner/due"), `TaskList`, `TaskDetail`, `StepChecklist`, `EvidenceCapture`, `OpeningClosingChecklist`, `DeptTaskGroup`, `SopCard`, `ShiftHandover`. - **Orchestration & exceptions:** `CreateTaskForm` (assign by store/region/role, evidence toggle), `EscalateDialog` (target + SLA), `EscalationThread`. - **Close-the-loop:** `ImpactSinceMorning`, `BeforeAfterStat`. - **Engine/data:** `signalsEngine.ts`, `kpi.ts`, `estateHealth.ts`, `sla.ts`, `seed/*`, `sources.ts`, `store.ts`, `useDemoReset()`. --- ## 10. Build order 1. **Scaffold** — Vite/React/TS, Tailwind tokens, Router, Zustand, GitHub Pages deploy a "hello" early (de-risk hosting). 2. **Types + seed** — entities; 12 stores/3 regions, ~24 products (PS5 line, TVs, phones, smart home, appliances), promos, inventory, KPIs, SOPs, sources. 3. **Engine** — signals→tasks rules, KPI/estate-health/SLA/impact calculators. Verify priorities appear from seed. 4. **Store tablet flow** (primary "aha") — daily brief → priority card → task detail → step checklist → **evidence capture** → complete → KPI ticks. 5. **Checklists + SOP + handover** — opening/closing, dept tasks, knowledge cards. 6. **Region cockpit** — league table, exceptions inbox, SLAs, store drilldown, coaching, **nudge/escalate**. 7. **HQ control tower + campaign centre** — estate health, theme tiles, signals explorer, compliance heatmap, adoption/lift. 8. **Analytics + Impact-since-morning** + **Sources panel** (F7). 9. **Task orchestration** — create/assign/recurring. 10. **Polish** — device frames, responsiveness, empty states, reset, deploy; tune seed so the PS5 story lands; rehearse the 15-min script. --- ## 11. 15-minute demo script 1. **(2m) HQ control tower** — estate health 82, 3 stores at risk, Promo theme dipping. PS5 bundle live in 12 stores; compliance heatmap shows 4 not converting, 3 displays unverified. 2. **(2m) Signals Explorer** — *"here's why the Copilot flagged it"* — the rule turning underperformance + unverified-display into prioritised tasks (trust/explainability). 3. **(4m) Store tablet (#214)** — daily brief → top priority *"Build & verify PS5 endcap — £3,200 at risk"* → work the checklist → **photo + stock-count evidence** → complete → KPI ticks. Glance at SOP card + opening checklist. 4. **(2m) Exception** — store #233 PS5 stock 0 / none on order → task auto-**Blocked → Escalated to Stock** with an **SLA countdown** + thread. 5. **(3m) Regional cockpit** — league table (#214 jumps), exceptions inbox + SLA chips, one-click **nudge** a lagging store, "stores needing coaching." 6. **(2m) Impact since morning** — +14 actions done, compliance 78→86%, 2 stores out of "at risk", £6.1k risk mitigated, DualSense attach-rate up. Close: execution → ROI. Open the **Sources panel** to show it's built on public category research. --- ## 12. Decisions to confirm before I build 1. **Brand name** — go with **"Voltly"**, or prefer *Currents / PowerHub / Brightwave Electricals*? (single swappable token either way) 2. **Scope depth** — full three personas as above (recommended), or go deepest on **Store + HQ** and keep Region lighter? 3. **UI kit** — plain Tailwind, or add **shadcn/ui** for a more enterprise-polished look? 4. **Ask Copilot box** — include the canned NL query, or keep the Signals Explorer as the only "AI" surface? 5. **Repo target** — build into this workspace folder and wire up GitHub Pages here? Confirm these (or just say "go with your recommendations") and I'll start Phase 0.
data.reasoningText:
The Argos fetch gave me actual useful data with real service terminology I can use for mock data—things like Fast Track delivery options, Click & Collect, Nectar loyalty points, and payment methods like Argos Pay and Klarna. This is much more grounded than what Currys returned.

I'm also noting the promotional mechanics they use—"Save £X" pricing, cashback offers, discount codes with patterns like LG10 and APP20, plus WOW deals and clearance sections. The brand roster (Samsung, LG, Hisense, Ring, Fitbit) and category structure (Technology, Gaming, Appliances, TV & soundbars, Health & beauty, Home & furniture) give me a realistic framework to work with.

For Currys, even though the fetch failed, I know from prior knowledge they have Team Knowhow for support services, their own cashback and trade-in programs, care plans, and ShopLive for one-to-one video shopping—which explains that Bambuser redirect I encountered. Now I have enough UK retail vocabulary and design patterns to build something authentic.

I'm thinking through the design system using those tokens the user provided—the indigo-blue accent at #464feb, light borders at #e6e6e6, and the subtle table header background. For a fictional retailer name that fits the modern electronics space without copying Currys or Argos directly, I'm considering options like Voltly, PowerHub, or Voltspark. I'll recommend one but keep alternatives ready.

The feature set needs to be exhaustive across those five operational themes—stock, promo, service, compliance, and colleague support—with a dashboard that shows estate health, at-risk stores, and overdue actions as the control tower. Rather than re-asking everything, I should present a tight, thorough revised plan and only ask for focused confirmations on a couple of key decisions.

Looking at what might be missing from the feature list: the user has "colleague support" as a theme but no explicit comms feature, so I should add a light huddle or shift handover capability with read receipts. The knowledge snippets and SOP cards are solid, and the evidence capture implied by the "evidence required toggle" should be a first-class feature for proof of execution.

For electronics retail specifically, I'm thinking about promo and planogram compliance (endcap displays, price label accuracy), stock operations (out-of-stock alerts on hot SKUs, click and collect pick SLA, replenishment workflows), and service ops tracking (care plan attach rates, repairs backlog, trade-in, customer wait times). Compliance and safety are critical too—age-restricted sales, electrical safety checks, fire and H&S, cash handling. A guided store-walk checklist could fold into compliance audits. I'll also add a coaching surface for regional managers, a recognition leaderboard, a notifications feed, and an audit trail to support the evidence and trust story.

I'm planning to present this as the core seven features expanded, plus electronics-specific operational scenarios, with a few recommended additions clearly marked and mapped back to the demo and market evidence so it's grounded in research, not just feature bloat.

For the source panel, I'll reference the actual public sources: Axonify's blog on retail execution software for the essential features taxonomy, Quorso for the missions and prioritized daily actions model, Currys and Argos for realistic electronics service operations and promo mechanics. I'll create a table showing what each source contributed, then use that to seed realistic mock data.

On the architecture side, I'm unifying AI priorities and manual tasks into a single Action entity with a source field, adding a Theme dimension to organize by stock, promo, service, and compliance, plus new entities for SOPs, evidence sources, and escalations with SLA tracking. I'll compute estate health scores and track impact deltas from a morning snapshot.

For the user experience, I'm thinking mobile-first for store managers and colleagues working in-aisle on tablets, with a separate desktop control tower for HQ. I'll add a persona switcher and optional device frames to show how the interface adapts—that really sells the "inside the stores" angle. On the AI framing, I'll keep the transparent rules engine but present it as an "AI prioritisation engine" with explainability through the "Why this matters" rationale, and maybe add a canned Ask Copilot feature as a stretch goal rather than full NL chat.

Now I'm structuring the deliverable with tables, architecture diagrams, and a design system section. I'll use the CSS tokens they provided—that indigo #464feb becomes the primary accent for links and active states—and build out a full palette with darker variants, neutrals, and status colors. I'm also deriving subtle color chips for the five operational themes (stock, promo, service, compliance, colleague) and settling on a clean system font stack for speed and familiarity.

For the mock data, I'm grounding it in real electronics retail scenarios: the PS5 Spider-Man 2 bundle as the anchor, plus seasonal promos like OLED TVs for football, air conditioners for warmer weather, smart home attachments, care plan upsells, and compliance edge cases like PEGI 18 age restrictions and staffing gaps on promo weekends. This gives the demo texture across all five themes and makes the signals feel authentic.

Now I'm mapping out the full structure: brand recommendation (leaning toward "Voltly" with alternatives like "Currents" or "PowerHub"), defining the estate health score as a weighted composite of completion, compliance, stock, and SLA metrics, and setting up the SLA mock data with target teams, time windows, and status calculations tied to a fixed demo timestamp so the escalations feel real and consistent.

For the demo flow, I'm seeding a morning snapshot of KPIs and then showing the impact as missions complete—tracking deltas like actions done, compliance improvements, stores moving out of risk, and financial risk mitigated. This closes the loop and shows tangible progress.

I'm mapping all seven of the user's requested features explicitly so nothing gets dropped, then layering in the additions, and I'll update the data model diagram to reflect everything.

The operating model — 14 domains → 5 pillars Every signal, task, KPI, SOP and filter is tagged with a **domain** (one of your 14) that rolls up to a **pillar** (one of 5). The control tower shows pillar tiles; you drill into domains. ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph A["People & Workforce — purple"] A1["1 People, HR & workforce admin"] A2["2 Scheduling, labour & shift ops"] A3["12 Training, coaching & performance"] end subgraph B["Trading & Execution — indigo"] B1["4 Opening, closing & daily trading"] B2["5 Task mgmt & operational execution"] B3["8 Merchandising, pricing & promotions"] end subgraph C["Stock & Fulfilment — blue"] C1["6 Stock, replenishment & back-of-house"] C2["7 Customer fulfilment (C&C, delivery)"] C3["13 Supplier & partner interactions"] end subgraph D["Risk, Safety & Compliance — amber"] D1["9 Quality, safety & regulatory"] D2["10 Loss prevention, security & risk"] D3["14 Customer service & returns"] end subgraph E["Enablement & Support — teal"] E1["3 Communications & knowledge"] E2["11 Equipment, IT & operational support"] end ``` This mapping is the answer to "is the feature set exhaustive?" — your 14 lists become the **domain catalogue**, and every individual line item (right-to-work check, temperature log, age-restricted refusal, label printing, etc.) becomes a **task template** seeded under its domain. --- ## 2. wattsRus Copilot — AI as a cross-cutting capability The Copilot is **one assistant, five skills**, reachable from a global launcher and embedded contextually. All deterministic/canned over seed data → works fully offline, demo-safe. | Skill | What it does | Used by | Domains touched | |---|---|---|---| | **Prioritise** | Turns signals from all 14 domains into a ranked daily action list — *why this matters · suggested action · expected impact · owner · due* | All | All | | **Ask (knowledge)** | NL Q&A over SOPs/policies: "How do I process a trade-in?", "What's the age-restricted refusal process?" → returns SOP card + source | Store / colleague | 3, 9, 10, 12 | | **Recommend (clienteling)** | Customer need → **in-stock** product matches with reasons + **attach** suggestions (accessories, care plan, trade-in). e.g. *"laptop for a student doing video editing, ~£700"* | Colleague (shop floor) | 8, 6, 14 | | **Draft / summarise** | Draft a store announcement, summarise a shift handover, summarise an exception for escalation | Store / Region | 3, 5, 11 | | **Explain** | Signals Explorer — shows the rule behind any priority (trust/explainability) | HQ / Region | All | The **Recommend** skill is the feature you flagged: it queries the same `Inventory` + `Product` seed, filters to in-stock at the current store, ranks by fit, and proposes attach items — turning the Copilot from an ops tool into a **customer-facing selling aid** too. --- ## 3. Demo scope — honest MVP cut 14 domains × full workflows is not a 15-minute demo. The architecture treats **all 14 as first-class** (data + nav), but we build a **vertical slice deep** and **represent the rest** with realistic seed data. | Domain | Demo level | Why | |---|---|---| | 5 Task mgmt & execution (+ evidence) | **Deep** | Core "aha": priority → steps → photo/count evidence → complete | | 8 Merchandising/pricing/promotions | **Deep** | Promo-execution storyline (display build, ticketing) | | 2 Scheduling / redeployment | **Deep** | Absence → real-time redeployment scenario | | 11 Equipment & IT support | **Deep** | Fridge/till repair → escalation + SLA | | 3 Comms & knowledge | **Deep** | SOP cards + Copilot "Ask" | | 6 Stock & replenishment | **Represented** | OOS signals, replenishment task, C&C linkage | | 7 Customer fulfilment | **Represented** | Click & Collect SLA + handover task | | 9 Quality/safety/compliance | **Represented** | Temperature log, fire check, age-restricted process | | 10 Loss prevention & risk | **Represented** | Age-restricted refusal log, shrink exception | | 12 Training & coaching | **Represented** | Mandatory-renewal-due signal, coaching list | | 1 People/HR, 13 Supplier, 14 Returns | **Catalogued** | Appear in taxonomy/nav + sample task templates; not deep | The **demo spine = one busy Saturday in June** (see §10) that walks through the Deep + Represented domains in a single narrative. --- ## 4. desktop cockpit"] -->|exceptions, SLAs, coaching, nudges| Store Store["Store Manager + Colleagues
in-store tablet / handheld"] -->|execute, capture evidence, escalate, clienteling| Region Region -->|signals & impact roll up| HQ ``` Persona switcher in the top bar (no real auth). **Reset demo** restores seed. --- ## 6. Routes / IA (HashRouter) | Route | Persona | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `/` | all | Persona switch / redirect | | `/hq` | HQ | Control tower — estate health, 5 pillar tiles, signals feed | | `/hq/campaign/:id` | HQ | Promo command centre (compliance heatmap, adoption, lift) | | `/hq/analytics` | HQ | Completion / compliance / stock-exception trends, coaching | | `/hq/signals` | HQ | Signals Explorer (Copilot "Explain") | | `/region` | Region | Cockpit: store league table + exceptions | | `/region/store/:id` | Region | Store drilldown + coaching | | `/region/escalations` | Region | Escalations inbox + SLA tracker | | `/store` | Store | **Daily brief + AI priorities** (tablet) | | `/store/task/:id` | Store | Task detail + steps + evidence | | `/store/checklists` | Store | Opening/closing + dept readiness | | `/store/workforce` | Store | Today's rota, absences, redeployment, handover | | `/store/knowledge` | Store | SOP / knowledge cards | | `/store/assist` | Colleague | **Copilot clienteling** (product recommendations) | | `/domains` | all | The 14-domain catalogue (browse all task templates) | | `/tasks/new` | HQ/Region | Create & assign task | | `/impact` | all | "Impact since morning" close-the-loop | Global top bar: persona switch · **Copilot launcher** · **Reset demo** · **Sources panel**. --- ## 9. Component breakdown - **Shell/shared:** `AppShell`, `TopBar`, `SideNav`, `DeviceFrame`, `CopilotLauncher`, `PillarChip`, `PriorityBadge`, `StatusPill`, `SlaChip`, `KpiStat`, `Sparkline`, `EvidenceThumb`, `SourceTag`, `EmptyState`. - **Copilot:** `CopilotPanel` (skill tabs), `AskKnowledge`, `ProductRecommender` (need → in-stock matches + attach), `DraftAssist`, `SignalsExplorer`. - **Control tower (HQ/Region):** `EstateHealthGauge`, `PillarHealthTiles`, `StoresAtRiskList`, `SignalsFeed`, `StoreLeagueTable`, `ComplianceHeatmap`, `ExceptionInbox`, `SlaTracker`, trend charts, `CoachingList`. - **Store (tablet):** `DailyBrief`, `PriorityCard`, `TaskList`, `TaskDetail`, `StepChecklist`, `EvidenceCapture`, `OpeningClosingChecklist`, `DeptReadiness`, `RotaToday`, `RedeployDialog`, `ShiftHandover`, `SopCard`. - **Orchestration/exceptions:** `CreateTaskForm`, `EscalateDialog`, `EscalationThread`, `DomainCatalogue`. - **Close-the-loop:** `ImpactSinceMorning`, `BeforeAfterStat`. - **Engine/data:** `signalsEngine.ts`, `kpi.ts`, `estateHealth.ts`, `sla.ts`, `copilot/*`, `seed/*`, `sources.ts`, `store.ts`, `useDemoReset()`. --- ## 10. Mock data & scenarios — "a busy Saturday in June" Diversified so no single product carries the demo. ~12 stores / 3 regions; ~30 products across **TV & Audio, Computing, Gaming, Mobile, Smart Home, Large Appliances**; multiple live promos and live operational signals: - **Promo execution:** major-sporting-event **TV promo** (football season) + a **gaming console bundle** + **back-to-school laptops** — displays unverified / not converting in some stores (D8). - **Stock:** heatwave driving **fan/portable-AC** demand → OOS signals + replenishment (D6); a hot SKU OOS with none on order → escalation. - **Fulfilment:** **Click & Collect** order aging past SLA → pick & stage (D7). - **Workforce:** a colleague **calls in sick** on a peak day → redeployment to the busy dept (D2); a **mandatory training renewal** due (D12); a **right-to-work/onboarding** task for a new starter (D1, catalogued). - **Equipment:** a **stockroom fridge / a till** faults → repair logged → **Facilities/IT escalation + SLA** (D11). - **Compliance/risk:** **temperature log** + **fire check** due (D9); an **age-restricted (PEGI 18) refusal** to log (D10). - **Service/clienteling:** a customer wants *"a laptop for a student doing video editing, ~£700"* → **Copilot Recommend** returns in-stock matches + care-plan/accessory attach (D8/D14). - **Familiar retail vocabulary** (from Currys/Argos research): Click & Collect / Fast Track, care plans / protection (Knowhow/Argos Care style), trade-in + cashback, price match, Nectar-style loyalty, returns & repairs. Seed is tuned so estate health, SLAs and the AM impact-snapshot all read correctly at the seeded "now". --- ## 11. Build order 1. **Scaffold** — Vite/React/TS, Tailwind tokens, Router, Zustand; deploy a "hello" to GitHub Pages early (de-risk hosting). 2. **Types + domain catalogue + seed** — pillars, 14 domains, task templates, 12 stores/3 regions, ~30 products, promos, inventory, KPIs, SOPs, colleagues/shifts, sources. 3. **Engine + calculators** — signals→tasks, KPI/estate-health/SLA/impact. Verify priorities appear. 4. **Store tablet spine (the aha)** — daily brief → priority card → task detail → steps → **evidence** → complete → KPI ticks. 5. **Copilot** — Prioritise + **Ask (knowledge)** + **Recommend (clienteling)** + Explain (Draft as stretch). 6. **Workforce + checklists** — rota/absence/redeploy/handover; opening/closing + dept readiness. 7. **Region cockpit** — league table, exceptions inbox, SLAs, drilldown, coaching, nudge/escalate. 8. **HQ control tower + campaign centre** — estate health, pillar tiles, signals explorer, compliance heatmap, adoption/lift. 9. **Analytics + Impact-since-morning + Sources panel + Domain catalogue.** 10. **Polish** — device frames, responsiveness, empty states, reset; tune seed; rehearse. --- ## 12. 15-minute talk track (draft — we'll refine later) 1. **(2m) HQ control tower** — estate health 82; 5 pillar tiles; Trading & Stock dipping; signals feed lighting up on a busy Saturday. 2. **(2m) Signals Explorer** — *"here's why the Copilot prioritised these"* — show one rule turning a promo + unverified-display signal into a ranked task (trust). 3. **(4m) Store tablet** — daily brief: top priorities ranked across domains. Work a **promo-display** task → steps → **photo + count evidence** → complete → KPI ticks. Glance at opening checklist + an SOP card. 4. **(2m) Copilot clienteling** — colleague asks for a **laptop recommendation**; Copilot returns in-stock matches + care-plan/accessory attach. ("Same assistant that runs your day also helps you sell.") 5. **(2m) Exception** — a fridge fault and an OOS-with-none-on-order both auto-**escalate** (Facilities / Stock) with **SLA countdowns**; a sick-call triggers a **redeployment** suggestion. 6. **(2m) Region + Impact** — league table, exceptions/SLA inbox, nudge a lagging store; then **Impact since morning** (actions done, compliance ↑, stores out of "at risk", £ risk mitigated). Open **Sources panel** to show it's built on public category research. --- ## 13. Source / Evidence panel (credibility artifact) Global slide-over listing the public sources and what each informed — so you can say *"based on public category research, not copied from any vendor."* | Source | Type | Informed | |---|---|---| | Axonify "Top 10 retail execution software 2026" | Public blog | Capability taxonomy: tasks/checklists, escalation, comms, training, mobile/offline, analytics; 5 execution challenges | | Quorso | Vendor site | "Missions" model: data → prioritised daily actions; Store/District/Central personas; close-the-loop impact | | Currys | Retailer site | Service ops vocabulary: Knowhow repairs/setup, ShopLive expert, care & repair, trade-in, price match | | Argos | Retailer site | Fast Track / Click & Collect, Argos Care protection, loyalty, BNPL, cashback, category & promo language | | Yoobic / Reflexis (Zebra) / GoSpotCheck / Repsly / StoreForce | Category leaders | Frontline comms, labour+task integration, photo evidence/compliance audits, league tables | | Your 14-domain task list | Provided requirements | The domain catalogue + task templates across People, Scheduling, Trading, Stock, Fulfilment, Merchandising, Safety, LP, IT, Training, Supplier, Returns | --- ## 14. Decisions to confirm before I build 1. **Scope cut (§3)** — happy with Deep on 5/8/2/11/3, Represented on 6/7/9/10/12, Catalogued for the rest? Or pull any domain up/down? 2. **Copilot skills** — build all of Prioritise + Ask + Recommend + Explain (Draft as stretch), or drop Draft entirely for time? 3. **UI kit** — plain Tailwind, or add shadcn/ui for a more enterprise-polished look? 4. **Brand lock** — wattsRus confirmed (logo as simple wordmark with the `#464feb` accent)? 5. **Repo target** — build into this workspace folder and wire GitHub Pages here? Say "go with your recommendations" (Deep slice as above, all Copilot skills except Draft, plain Tailwind + light shadcn, wattsRus, this folder) and I'll switch to build mode and start Phase 0.
data.reasoningText:
I need to revise my approach based on the full scope of store operations they've outlined. The PS5 example was just illustrative, so I should use a broader range of electronics retail scenarios instead of fixating on that one product. The company is wattsRus, not Voltly. I'm also realizing the AI chat assistant has broader potential—it could handle product recommendations when customers ask what's available, not just promotional announcements, making it a more versatile tool across different operational areas.

Given the 15-minute demo constraint, I can't build all 14 categories deeply. Instead, I should map out the full domain model showing all 14 as operational pillars, then select a focused vertical slice to actually implement while representing the others through navigation and data. The 14 categories naturally cluster into broader pillars: People & Workforce (HR, scheduling, training), Trading & Execution (opening/closing, task management, merchandising), Stock & Fulfilment (inventory, customer orders, supplier management), Risk & Compliance (safety, loss prevention, customer service), and Support & Communications (knowledge base, equipment management).

The wattsRus Copilot becomes a cross-cutting AI assistant with multiple capabilities: ranking daily priorities from signals, answering operational questions and SOP lookups, recommending products for customers, and drafting communications. toolRequests.arguments:
{"content": "// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// wattsRus Store Operations Copilot — domain types
// All data is synthetic. No backend. See /src/data for the seeded estate.
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export type Role = 'HQ' | 'Regional' | 'Store'

/** The 5 operational pillars that group the 14 domains. */
export type PillarId = 'people' | 'trading' | 'stock' | 'risk' | 'enablement'

/** The 14 operational task domains (the canonical operating model). */
export type DomainId =
  | 'people-hr' // 1  People, HR & workforce admin
  | 'scheduling' // 2  Scheduling, labour & shift ops
  | 'comms-knowledge' // 3  Communications & knowledge access
  | 'opening-closing' // 4  Opening, closing & daily trading
  | 'task-execution' // 5  Task management & operational execution
  | 'stock-replenishment' // 6  Stock, replenishment & back-of-house
  | 'fulfilment' // 7  Customer fulfilment (C&C, delivery)
  | 'merchandising' // 8  Merchandising, pricing & promotions
  | 'safety-compliance' // 9  Quality, safety & regulatory compliance
  | 'loss-prevention' // 10 Loss prevention, security & risk
  | 'equipment-it' // 11 Equipment, IT & operational support
  | 'training-coaching' // 12 Training, coaching & performance
  | 'supplier' // 13 Supplier & partner interactions
  | 'returns-service' // 14 Customer service & returns

export type DemoDepth = 'deep' | 'represented' | 'catalogued'

export interface Pillar {
  id: PillarId
  name: string
  /** CSS token name, e.g. "pillar-people" → bg-pillar-people */
  color: string
  blurb: string
  domainIds: DomainId[]
}

export interface Domain {
  id: DomainId
  /** 1–14, matches the requirements list ordering. */
  index: number
  name: string
  pillarId: PillarId
  depth: DemoDepth
  blurb: string
  /** Representative line items from the requirements (used by the catalogue). */
  examples: string[]
}

// ── Org & estate ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface Region {
  id: string
  name: string
  managerUserId: string
}

export type StoreFormat = 'Superstore' | 'High Street' | 'Retail Park' | 'Outlet'

export interface Store {
  id: string
  /** Branch number shown to users, e.g. "214". */
  code: string
  name: string
  town: string
  regionId: string
  format: StoreFormat
  managerUserId: string
  departments: Department[]
}

export type Department =
  | 'TV & Audio'
  | 'Computing'
  | 'Gaming'
  | 'Mobile & Wearables'
  | 'Smart Home'
  | 'Large Appliances'
  | 'Customer Service'

export interface User {
  id: string
  name: string
  role: Role
  jobTitle: string
  regionId?: string
  storeId?: string
  initials: string
}

// ── Catalogue, promotions & inventory ───────────────────────────────────────

export type ProductCategory = Department

export interface Product {
  id: string
  sku: string
  name: string
  brand: string
  category: ProductCategory
  price: number
  /** Short merchandising blurb used by the Copilot recommender. */
  blurb: string
  /** Free-text tags for clienteling matches (e.g. "student", "video editing"). */
  tags: string[]

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// wattsRus Store Operations Copilot — domain types
// All data is synthetic. No backend. See /src/data for the seeded estate.
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export type Role = 'HQ' | 'Regional' | 'Store'

/** The 5 operational pillars that group the 14 domains. */
export type PillarId = 'people' | 'trading' | 'stock' | 'risk' | 'enablement'

/** The 14 operational task domains (the canonical operating model). */
export type DomainId =
  | 'people-hr' // 1  People, HR & workforce admin
  | 'scheduling' // 2  Scheduling, labour & shift ops
  | 'comms-knowledge' // 3  Communications & knowledge access
  | 'opening-closing' // 4  Opening, closing & daily trading
  | 'task-execution' // 5  Task management & operational execution
  | 'stock-replenishment' // 6  Stock, replenishment & back-of-house
  | 'fulfilment' // 7  Customer fulfilment (C&C, delivery)
  | 'merchandising' // 8  Merchandising, pricing & promotions
  | 'safety-compliance' // 9  Quality, safety & regulatory compliance
  | 'loss-prevention' // 10 Loss prevention, security & risk
  | 'equipment-it' // 11 Equipment, IT & operational support
  | 'training-coaching' // 12 Training, coaching & performance
  | 'supplier' // 13 Supplier & partner interactions
  | 'returns-service' // 14 Customer service & returns

export type DemoDepth = 'deep' | 'represented' | 'catalogued'

export interface Pillar {
  id: PillarId
  name: string
  /** CSS token name, e.g. "pillar-people" → bg-pillar-people */
  color: string
  blurb: string
  domainIds: DomainId[]
}

export interface Domain {
  id: DomainId
  /** 1–14, matches the requirements list ordering. */
  index: number
  name: string
  pillarId: PillarId
  depth: DemoDepth
  blurb: string
  /** Representative line items from the requirements (used by the catalogue). */
  examples: string[]
}

  id: string
  name: string
  managerUserId: string

  code: string
  name: string
  town: string
  regionId: string
  format: StoreFormat
  managerUserId: string
  departments: Department[]
}

  | 'Computing'
  | 'Gaming'
  | 'Mobile & Wearables'
  | 'Smart Home'
  | 'Large Appliances'
  | 'Customer Service'

>

export const PILLAR_BY_ID = Object.fromEntries(PILLARS.map((p) => [p.id, p])) as Record<

>

For store 301 in London Stratford, I'm making the console bundle out of stock with nothing on order to trigger an escalation. import type { StoreKpi } from '@/types'

// Explicit per-store KPIs so the league table, estate-health score and the
// "impact since morning" deltas all read intentionally during the demo.
// `morning` is the start-of-day snapshot; live values reflect the trading day.
export const STORE_KPIS: StoreKpi[] = [
  // North
  { storeId: 's-214', salesVsTargetPct: 96, conversionPct: 23, attachRatePct: 31, carePlanAttachPct: 18, oosRatePct: 6, compliancePct: 82, csat: 88, morning: { compliancePct: 78, attachRatePct: 29, conversionPct: 22 } },
  { storeId: 's-118', salesVsTargetPct: 104, conversionPct: 27, attachRatePct: 38, carePlanAttachPct: 24, oosRatePct: 4, compliancePct: 91, csat: 90, morning: { compliancePct: 90, attachRatePct: 37, conversionPct: 26 } },

  { storeId: 's-211', salesVsTargetPct: 99, conversionPct: 24, attachRatePct: 33, carePlanAttachPct: 19, oosRatePct: 6, compliancePct: 86, csat: 87, morning: { compliancePct: 85, attachRatePct: 33, conversionPct: 24 } },

  { storeId: 's-228', salesVsTargetPct: 81, conversionPct: 18, attachRatePct: 24, carePlanAttachPct: 12, oosRatePct: 12, compliancePct: 66, csat: 78, morning: { compliancePct: 66, attachRatePct: 24, conversionPct: 18 } },
